# Cross-Channel Marketing: A Beginner's Guide - Iterable

## Cross-Channel Marketing: A Beginner’s Guide

**Published by**

April 24, 2025

![Overhead view of woman standing using her phone. We see a screen full of apps and in the background, her crossed sneakers on the ground behind it.](https://iterable.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/X-Channel-Guide_Blog-Header.png)

### The Death of Batch-and-blast Marketing

We’ve entered a new era of moments-based marketing, one where the old-school campaign calendar just doesn’t cut it anymore. Today’s consumers are in the driver’s seat, navigating their own paths across email, SMS, mobile apps, and more—on their own time.

Rigid, batch-and-blast campaigns can’t match the pace or demands of a customer who moves fluidly from one channel to the next, expecting personalized, frictionless interactions at every touchpoint. That’s why forward-thinking marketers are abandoning stuffy campaign schedules in favor of intelligent, always-on orchestration.

It’s time to move beyond the one-size-fits-all playbook and adopt a new approach built around real-time, customer-led journeys. Modern marketing isn’t about managing channels in silos—it’s about creating a connected experience that meets your audience wherever they are.

> _Cross-channel shoppers generate 30% higher lifetime value than single-channel buyers._

### The Power of a Connected Cross-channel Strategy

Using more channels doesn’t automatically mean better engagement—it’s how they work together that makes the difference. Moments-based marketing is about seamlessly connecting with each customer like they’re your only customer. With a cross-channel strategy and the right tools, every interaction builds on the last—creating a frictionless flow from first touch to conversion.

> _Brands using three or more channels in a campaign see a 287% higher purchase rate compared to single-channel marketing._

When email, SMS, push, and in-app messaging work in sync, customers don’t just receive messages—they experience a consistent, intuitive journey. A centralized, cohesive strategy ensures that every touchpoint is relevant, timely, and designed to move customers forward—driving stronger engagement, retention, and lifetime value.

**Channel**

**How to Utilize?**

**When to Utilize?**

**What Format to Utilize?**

Email

For providing context and direction around core product and brand messages with ease & efficiency.

– Transactional updates  
– Customer support  
– Milestone celebrations  
– Brand/product news

Text, images, files, and links

SMS/MMS

For driving urgency through brief, real-time messages that inspire relevant action.

– Exclusive offers

– Flash sales

– Confirmations

– Reminders

– Personalized win-back messages

SMS: Short text (160 characters)

MMS: Text, images, videos, audio

In-App Messaging

For optimizing engagement and conversion funnel metrics with unique experiences rooted in customer signals.

– Behavior-based recommendations  
– Guided tutorials  
– Feature discovery  
– Gamification

Text, banners, and pop-ups

In-Browser Messaging

For capturing visitor attention at key moments while they actively browse, without requiring them to leave the page.

– Promotional offers  
– Feature announcements  
– Cart abandonment  
– Product recommendations

Banners, modals, and pop-ups

Web Push Notifications

For re-engaging users outside of the website with their permission, bringing them back to your site with a single click.

– Flash sales  
– Order notifications  
– Cart abandonment  
– Event reminders

Short, clickable alerts, sometimes with images or rich media

Mobile Push Notifications

For activating a passive audience with context-aware prompts that feel natural and valuable.

– Limited deals  
– Subscription alerts  
– Re-engagement prompts

Short text alerts with CTA notifications, sometimes with rich media

Embedded Messaging

For delivering native, context-aware messages within mobile and web apps without disrupting the user experience.

– Helpful nudges  
– Personalized content  
– Milestone celebrations

Inline notifications, embedded banners, or dynamic modules

WhatsApp

For engaging users on a platform they frequently use, ensuring interactive, high-visibility conversations.

– Customer support  
– Product launches  
– Personalized offers  
– Order updates

Rich media and interactive elements like buttons and catalogs

OTT Messaging

For delivering non-intrusive messaging in a highly visual and immersive format within streaming platforms.

– Marketing campaigns  
– Localized promotions  
– Subscriber boost

Video, interactive overlays, branded ads, and deep-link notifications

### AI-powered Channel Optimization

Coordinating multiple channels is only the beginning—real cross-channel success lies in how they’re orchestrated to work together seamlessly. Without a clear, connected strategy, brands risk overwhelming customers, missing key touchpoints, or delivering disjointed experiences.

That’s where Iterable Nova comes in. Powered by Iterable’s AI engine, Nova enables modern marketers to move beyond guesswork with smart, goal-based guidance. It’s intuitive, conversational, and built to help you turn big-picture ideas into meaningful, measurable results—no matter the medium.

In addition to Nova serving as your intelligent assistant, Iterable’s full AI Suite automatically analyzes historical data and sends messages when, where, and how often customers want to engage with them.

Here are three ways Iterable empowers marketers to meet their audiences wherever they are.

*   **Channel Optimization**: Like real estate, marketing channels are all about location, location, location. Iterable’s Channel Optimization analyzes user engagement patterns to determine the most effective channel—email, SMS, or push—for each message, ensuring increased engagement and higher conversions.
*   **Frequency Optimization**: Too many messages drown customers in unnecessary noise, but too few leave money on the table. Frequency Optimization dynamically adjusts message volume based on user preferences and engagement trends, helping brands strike the right balance.
*   **Send Time Optimization**: No more blasting emails at 9 a.m. on Tuesdays. Send Time Optimization uses machine learning to predict when each user is most likely to engage, delivering messages at the moments that matter most.
